column1: "* **Supervised Learning:** The algorithm learns from labeled data. This means the dataset includes both the input features and the correct output targets. The model learns to map inputs to the correct output.\n"
"  * *Real-World Use Case:* Predicting crop yield or diagnosing whether a medical scan shows a benign or malignant growth.\n"
"* **Unsupervised Learning:** The algorithm works with unlabeled data. The goal is to discover hidden patterns
column3: or groupings within the data without any explicit guidance.\n"

column1: "  * *Real-World Use Case:* Segmenting customers based on purchasing behavior or grouping similar soil types based on raw sensor telemetry."
"# (Using a standard structured setup matching the format of previous weeks)\n"

column2: we split the data into a **Training Set** (typically 70-80%) to teach the model
column3: and a **Testing Set** (20-30%) kept completely hidden. \n"

column1: "Testing the model on unseen data ensures we get an honest assessment of how the model will perform after deployment in the real world. Testing it on training data would be like giving a student an exam using the exact questions they memorized answers for."
"* **Overfitting (High Variance):** The model learns the training data *too well*
column2: memorizing its specific noise and random anomalies. \n"

column1: "  * *Effect:* Extremely high accuracy on training data
column2: but poor performance on new test data.\n"

column1: "* **Underfitting (High Bias):** The model is too simple to capture the underlying pattern in the data.\n"
"  * *Effect:* Poor performance on both the training data and the test data.\n"

column2: we use multiple metrics to evaluate performance:\n"

column1: "* **Confusion Matrix:** A table tracking True Positives (TP)
"* **Precision:** Out of everything the model claimed was positive
column2: how much was actually positive? ($\\frac{TP}{TP + FP}$)\n"

column1: "* **Recall:** Out of all actual positives available
column2: how many did the model successfully find? ($\\frac{TP}{TP + FN}$)"
